title: Terebinthus morelensis
intro: Terebinthus morelensis, also known as Pistacia lentiscus, is a small, evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Terebinthus morelensis include terpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, such as pistacigenin and lentiginoside, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Terebinthus morelensis is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or oil, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
